question:
What is the evaluation criteria for Applied Machine
  Learning in Health Care?
answer:
The evaluation criteria for Applied Machine Learning in Health Care involves a relative grading method, where the final grade will be based on the marks obtained in various assessment components, including quizzes, assignments, mid-term presentations, and a project, with relative weights of 20%, 20%, 20%, and 40% respectively.

This evaluation criteria is supported by the Evaluation Summary section of the course handout, which outlines the relative weights and schedules for each assessment component, as well as the rubrics and remarks for each component. The specific details of the evaluation criteria can be found in the 2025_course_handout_AMCH.pdf file.

The final grade will be based on the marks obtained in various assessment components defined in the assessment table below. Relative grading method defined in the academic regulations of the university will be followed to grade the students. Evaluation Component Weightage (%) Evaluation Schedule Rubrics/Remarks Quiz 20 (2 quizzes each of weightage 10%) February 23, 2026 April 16, 2026 Each quiz is a 20-minute-long closed-book assessment component. Understanding of Concepts (40%) ● Epidemiological concepts such as incidence, prevalence, compartmental models, and time-series basics. ● Validation of students’ understanding through timed quiz. Problem Solving (60%) ● Application of data analysis, modeling, and forecasting techniques. Even Semester AY 2025-26 Page 6 of 8 ©BMU ● Logical ability to problem-solving skill in atimely manner. Assignment 20 (1 assignment of weightage 20%) April 10, 2026 One open-book assignment of weight 10%. Understanding of Concepts (20%) ● Detailed understanding of epidemiological data structures, modeling assumptions, and forecasting methods. ● Ability to interpret outputs from computational tools. Timeliness (20%) ● Assignment must be submitted on or before the due date and time. Problem Solving (60%) ● Application of computational and statistical methods to real-world public health datasets. ● Interpretation and discussion of analytical results. Mid-Term Presentation (Equivalent to Mid-Term Examination) 20 March 29, 2026 (Presentation tentatively between March 30-April 03 2026) Understanding of Concepts (20%) ● Clarity in explaining epidemiological concepts, data sources, and modeling approach. ● Ability to relate theory with real-world disease data. Timeliness (10%) ● Presentation submission on or before the specified deadline. Problem Solving (20%) ● Demonstration of coding skills. ● Visualization of the problems using software. Presentation and Viva (50%) ● Logical structure, clarity of slides, and effective communication. ● Developing presentation skills to present the work in agroup. ● Demonstration of individual knowledge through viva. Project 40 April 22 2026 Understanding of Concepts (10%) Even Semester AY 2025-26 Page 7 of 8 ©BMU (Equivalent to End-Term Examination) (Presentation tentative April 23 -May 1 2026) ● Comprehensive understanding of epidemiological theory and computational techniques. Timeliness (10%) ● Presentation submission on or before the specified deadline. Problem Solving (20%) ● Demonstration of coding skills. ● Visualization of the problems using software. Presentation and Viva (50%) ● Logical structure, clarity of slides, and effective communication. ● Developing presentation skills to present the work in a group. ● Demonstration of individual knowledge through viva. Project 40 April 22 2026 Understanding of Concepts (10%) Even Semester AY 2025-26 Page 7 of 8 ©BMU (Equivalent to End-Term Examination) (Presentation tentative April 23 -May 1 2026) ● Comprehensive understanding of epidemiological theory and computational techniques. Timeliness (10%) ● Submission of project report and code within the stipulated deadline. Report Writing and Problem Solving (30%) ● Preparation of a structured technical report. ● Application of modeling, forecasting, and data analysis methods to a real disease dataset. ● Demonstration of programming skills and result interpretation. Presentation and Viva (50%) ● Clear presentation of methodology, results, and conclusions. ● Demonstration of individual contribution and understanding through viva. ● Ability to apply concepts to solve complex problems Experiential Learning Components: Hands-on projects on applications of Computational Epidemiology and Public Health Data Analytics using Python will be given to the students. Students will work in groups on real-world public health datasets involving epidemiological data wrangling, implementation of compartmental epidemic models, parameter estimation, time-series forecasting, and visualization of disease trends through dashboards. This entire set of experiential learning activities will be assessed through the evaluation component carrying a weightage of 60%. Student Responsibilities: ● Attend lectures to obtain all the course material that you are responsible for. ● Check announcements at Maitri/Google Classroom on a regular basis. ● Submit assignments/projects on time. Regularly, check your marks on the Maitri and inform the instructor in-charge in case of any issues. Attendance Policy: Students are expected to attend the classes regularly. Course Outcomes: At the end of the course, the students should be able to: Course Outcome Statement Mapped Component Associated Activities / Assessment CO1: Understand the fundamentals of public health data and epidemiological concepts including line lists, incidence and prevalence measures, chain of infection, biological data handling, time-series structuring, smoothing techniques, and spatial visualization using incidence maps and choropleth maps. Skill Development Quiz, Assignment, Mid-Term Presentation & End-Term Presentation for project work CO2: Apply data wrangling, visualization, and computational techniques for real-world epidemiological datasets, fetch live public health data, construct time-series datasets, and implement compartmental epidemic models with parameter estimation and reproduction number analysis. Skill Development Quiz, Assignment Mid-Term Presentation & End-Term Presentation for project work CO3: Analyze epidemic dynamics using compartmental models, time-series analysis, forecasting techniques, regression models, and evaluate Skill Development Employability Assignment Mid-Term Presentation & End-Term Presentation Even Semester AY 2025-26 Page 3 of 8 ©BMU model performance using validation metrics, residual diagnostics, and comparative analysis between classical and data-driven models. for project work CO4: Evaluate and formulate real-world infectious disease problems by integrating epidemiological theory, statistical modeling, time-series forecasting, and neural networks to develop predictive dashboards and decision-support tools for public health applications.
